summary: S37 confirmed the recurring pattern across v2.7.1-3: narrative MANDATORY rules in SKILL.md get skipped 100% under load, mechanical hooks/wrappers hold 100%. Architectural conclusion formalized as PhantomOS master doctrine. PhantomOS reasons over a business universe, does not fill forms. Two-tier rule: mechanical layer (mutations, schemas, paths, destructive ops) → strict enforcement, hooks, refusals; semantic layer (audience, tone, positioning, recommendations, narrative claims) → strict trust, model reasons, operator iterates. Decisive test for any new rule/hook/gate: prevents destructive persistent error, or pre-validates the model's reasoning? Latter → strip. 7 named anti-patterns banned (form-fill agent, path leak, confirmation theater, scope creep on confirm, schema-completeness fetishism, stack-trace-as-explanation, validation cascade).

changes.reason: Canonical doctrine doc. Thesis, two-tier rule, decisive test, 7 anti-patterns with examples.
New 'Master doctrine — Contextual intelligence' section at top, before FIRST ACTION. Two-tier rule + decisive test surfaced runtime.
Core principles section opens with contextual-intelligence reference — every voice rule serves it.

operator_impact: None directly. All future architectural decisions must pass the decisive test before ship. Trust-first on semantics is now binding doctrine, not implicit preference.
tested_on: phantom-stress sync — doctrine readable from CLAUDE.md root at session start
caught_by: Cumulative pattern across v2.6.17 → v2.7.3 — soft prescriptions decay under load, mechanical primitives hold.
known_gaps: Doctrine compliance is itself enforced semantically (read at session start). No hook checks that new skills pass the decisive test — relies on operator gate during skill creation.
